name: Montes de Valdorba – circular desde Pueyo por el cerro San Pelaio
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Pueyo traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and agricultural plains, offering varied terrain for cycling. The region features a mix of open countryside and wooded sections, with routes often connecting historical towns and natural viewpoints. Mountain biking in Pueyo provides opportunities to explore the rural Spanish landscape, with routes suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Pueyo The most popular mountain bike trail is Tafalla and Olite Royal Palace Loop, a 16.7 miles (26.9 km) trail that takes about 2 hours to complete. This route offers views of historical architecture and the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Montes de Valdorba – circular desde Pueyo por el cerro San Pelaio, a moderate 17.8 miles (28.7 km) path. This trail leads through the Valdorba hills, including an ascent to Cerro San Pelaio. Local mountain bikers also love the Hermitage of Santa Zita – Molino de Olleta loop from Tafalla, a 25.9 miles (41.7 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and past the Hermitage of Santa Zita, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Mountain biking around Pueyo is defined by rolling hills, open fields, and scattered woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Pueyo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 25 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Pueyo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Pueyo?
A: Pueyo offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 110 routes available. These range from easy rides to more challenging paths, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Pueyo?
A: The terrain in Pueyo is characterized by rolling hills and agricultural plains, offering a mix of open countryside and wooded sections. You'll find varied surfaces, from smooth paths to more rugged sections, especially on trails that ascend to viewpoints like Cerro San Pelaio.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Pueyo?
A: Yes, Pueyo has several easy mountain bike trails. There are 12 routes specifically graded as easy, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride through the rural landscape.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Pueyo?
A: Many of Pueyo's mountain bike routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988413495/montes-de-valdorba-circular-desde-pueyo-por-el-cerro-san-pelaio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Valdorba – circular desde Pueyo por el cerro San Pelaio&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate loop that takes you through the Valdorba hills.

Q: What are some scenic mountain bike routes with good viewpoints in Pueyo?
A: For scenic views, consider routes that include ascents to local hills. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988413495/montes-de-valdorba-circular-desde-pueyo-por-el-cerro-san-pelaio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Valdorba – circular desde Pueyo por el cerro San Pelaio&lt;/a&gt; offers vistas from Cerro San Pelaio. Additionally, many routes connect historical towns and offer panoramic views of the surrounding countryside.

Q: What landmarks or attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails in Pueyo?
A: Several routes pass by historical and natural attractions. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2704481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Olite&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6250557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María la Real&lt;/a&gt;, or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6251952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Olive Tree of Olite&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Zita – Molino de Olleta loop from Tafalla&lt;/a&gt; also takes you past the historic Hermitage of Santa Zita.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Pueyo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Pueyo are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 25 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the ability to connect historical towns, and the opportunities to explore the rural Spanish landscape.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that pass through historical towns?
A: Yes, many routes in Pueyo are designed to connect historical towns. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988407444/tafalla-and-olite-royal-palace-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tafalla and Olite Royal Palace Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering views of historical architecture as you cycle through the countryside.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Pueyo?
A: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Pueyo, with mild temperatures and less extreme weather. Summer can be hot, while winter might bring cooler temperatures, but trails are often accessible year-round.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for advanced riders in Pueyo?
A: Yes, for experienced riders, Pueyo offers 14 difficult mountain bike trails. These routes typically feature more significant elevation gains and technically demanding sections, providing a good challenge for advanced mountain bikers.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that are longer distances in Pueyo?
A: Pueyo offers a range of route lengths. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Zita – Molino de Olleta loop from Tafalla&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 41.7 km (25.9 miles) trail, providing a longer ride through rural landscapes.
tips: This humble but picturesque hermitage is located in the heart of the Montes de Valdorba and on the top of Cerro Pelaio at 959 meters above sea level.

This small but very picturesque town in the heart of Monte de Valdorba will give you the feeling of traveling back in time. It is surrounded by a wonderful landscape and its stone houses, many abandoned, make a beautiful postcard to remember.
